description_long: This object is a large, pole-mounted solar energy array consisting of two rectangular grids of photovoltaic panels. Each grid is composed of multiple blue-black solar cells arranged in a precise matrix, supported by a robust metal framework. The panels are elevated above the ground by a central cylindrical pole, which branches out into angled metal supports that hold the grids securely. The structure appears engineered for stability and optimal sunlight exposure, with the panels tilted at a fixed angle. The metal framework and pole are likely made of steel or aluminum, providing both strength and resistance to weather. The overall design is utilitarian and modern, suitable for installation in open urban spaces such as parks, parking lots, or community centers.
description: A large, pole-mounted solar array with two rectangular grids of blue-black photovoltaic panels supported by a sturdy metal frame. The panels are elevated and tilted for optimal sunlight capture.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full expanse of the two rectangular solar panel grids, with the central supporting pole visible at the bottom.
description_view_1: Angled side view revealing the depth of the panel grids and the triangular metal support structure beneath each grid.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the dual-grid configuration and the branching metal supports connecting the grids to the central pole.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view, similar to view 1, showing the underside framework and the cylindrical pole.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 25 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: pole-mounted with steel or aluminum support arms and a central cylindrical pole
attribute_array_configuration: two rectangular grids, each approximately 3x4 panels, mounted side by side on a central pole
